summary: CI/package maintenance PR resolving #1102. It re-enabled the package publishing workflow, replaced the MEGA compiler download with files.decomp.dev plus curl, adjusted the gen-pages container to copy the packaged virtualenv into the checkout before activation, and temporarily removed the editable tools/asm-differ dependency from requirements generation.
what_changed: Changed MELEE_COMPILERS_URL in the build-linux Dockerfile from a mega.nz URL to https://files.decomp.dev/compilers_latest.zip.
Replaced the build-linux setup dependency on megatools with curl while keeping libarchive-tools, and changed compiler installation from megadl piping into bsdtar to curl -L piping into bsdtar.
Adjusted gen-pages entrypoint to copy /opt/venv into the checked-out repository as .venv and activate .venv/bin/activate instead of directly activating /opt/venv.
Removed the `if: false` guard from .github/workflows/publish-packages.yml, allowing the build-linux package publishing job to run again.
Commented out the editable asm-differ entry in requirements.in as `# TODO -e file:tools/asm-differ#egg=asm-differ`.

Regenerated requirements.txt with `pip-compile --strip-extras requirements.in`, removing asm-differ and its transitive pinned dependencies such as ansiwrap, cxxfilt, levenshtein, rapidfuzz, textwrap3, and watchdog.
smart_moves: Moved the compiler artifact download away from MEGA tooling to a direct files.decomp.dev URL, simplifying the Docker image dependency set and avoiding reliance on megatools.
Used curl with `-L` for the compiler zip download, preserving redirect handling for the hosted `compilers_latest.zip` URL.
Kept extraction streamed through bsdtar, so the setup script still avoids a separate temporary downloaded archive file.

Copied the container virtualenv into the cloned input tree for gen-pages before installing repository requirements, which likely avoids path or mutability issues from installing directly into the shared /opt/venv.
Regenerated requirements.txt after modifying requirements.in, keeping the lock file consistent with the source dependency list.
decomp_lessons: For CI images, prefer stable project-controlled artifact hosting over external consumer file hosts when compiler bundles are required.
Package-building Dockerfiles should minimize specialized download clients when a standard tool such as curl can fetch the artifact.
When removing or disabling local editable dependencies from requirements.in, regenerate requirements.txt immediately so transitive dependencies are not left behind.
If a packaged venv is used as a base for documentation or pages generation, copying it into the working tree before per-repo installs can isolate the packaged environment from job-local mutations.
Avoid leaving CI package publishing disabled with hardcoded workflow guards once the underlying package issues are fixed.
naming_conventions: Compiler bundle environment variable is named `MELEE_COMPILERS_URL`.
The extracted `GC` compiler directory is renamed to `mwcc_compiler` and installed under `/opt`.
The gen-pages job uses a repository-local `.venv` after copying from `/opt/venv`.
The workflow job is named `build-linux` with display name `Publish package`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body only states `Resolves #1102.`, so the exact failure mode from that issue is not visible in this slice.
follow_up_queries: Find issue #1102 to understand the exact CI package failure this PR resolved.
Search later PRs for re-enabling or replacing the TODO asm-differ editable dependency in requirements.in.
Check package workflow runs after PR #1247 to confirm the publish-packages job succeeded with files.decomp.dev and curl.
Search for later changes to MELEE_COMPILERS_URL or compilers_latest.zip hosting.
